findings.issue: Task 10 (documentation-writing) lists reviewer `dev-code-reviewer`, which matches the catalog default for documentation-writing. No issue. Noted only for completeness: Task 11 (pre-deploy-qa) has `Reviewers: none`, which is correct per the catalog (QA is its own verification).

summary: Tech-spec is template-compliant and ready for implementation. All required sections are present and non-empty (Solution, Architecture with both subsections, Decisions with Decision+Rationale+Alternatives each, Data Models with explicit reuse statement, Dependencies with New/Using-existing, Testing Strategy with Feature size: M, Agent Verification Plan with all three subsections, Risks as a Risk/Mitigation table, Acceptance Criteria, Implementation Tasks by waves). Frontmatter is valid (created 2026-06-22 YYYY-MM-DD, status draft, branch feature/record-report-0-11-views filled, size M). All 11 tasks carry Skill + Reviewers (except the QA task, correctly none) + Verify + Files to modify/read. Skills (code-writing, documentation-writing, pre-deploy-qa) and reviewers (dev-code-reviewer, dev-test-reviewer) are all valid per the catalog and exist on disk; no LLM-prompt work mis-assigned to code-writing. Task descriptions are brief scope statements (<=5 sentences, WHAT+WHY), with no embedded AC/TDD-anchor headings, no line-number references, and no decision-like rationale leaking out of the Decisions section. The Final Wave contains a Pre-deploy QA task. Verification steps are concrete bash/go-test commands (no abstract 'verify it works'); verify is present on all tasks with observable output. No time-based estimates anywhere. Wave conflict detection: no two tasks in the same wave modify the same file (Wave 2 per-screen golden tests are isolated to separate _test.go files and distinct testdata goldens; report/report.go is touched by Task 2 in Wave 1 and Task 9 in Wave 3 — different waves, no parallel conflict, and the Risks table explicitly sequences this). Task dependencies respect wave ordering (Wave 2 and 3 declared 'after Wave 1'); no circular dependencies. Task count is 11 (<=15). File paths and naming (internal/view, cmd/report, report/, internal/stat, report/testdata) match architecture.md and patterns.md conventions, including the documented count-test coupling (Test_filterViews, view_test, TestNew). Zero critical findings -> approved.
